title: Quality, parity, and operator readiness
description:
Turn the design into something safe to implement and operate by covering deterministic tests, degraded-path tests, parity checks, docs, and runbook material. This epic is where implementation confidence gets locked in.

## Success Criteria
- core behaviors are test-backed
- operator guidance exists
- parity regressions are easy to detect

## Notes
- Focus on revoked/suspended/degraded behavior, adaptation bounds, and parity
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- core behaviors are test-backed
- operator guidance exists
- parity regressions are easy to detect
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Focus on revoked/suspended/degraded behavior, adaptation bounds, and parity

title: Add unit tests for mode resolution, normalization, and profile derivation
description:
Write focused unit tests for the core deterministic pieces: normalized input handling, compose mode resolution, bounded profile derivation, and key rule generation. These tests should isolate logic so regressions are easy to pinpoint.

## Acceptance Criteria
- core deterministic helpers have unit tests
- revoked/suspended/degraded cases are covered
- drift clamping is exercised

## Notes
- Keep tests small and table-driven where possible
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- core deterministic helpers have unit tests
- revoked/suspended/degraded cases are covered
- drift clamping is exercised
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep tests small and table-driven where possible

title: Add integration tests for suspended, revoked, degraded, and baseline-only composition
description:
Create end-to-end service-level tests for the main behavioral modes and their warning surfaces. This task ensures the composed outputs reflect the plan rather than only individual helper expectations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- service-level tests cover major modes
- warnings and status summaries are asserted
- fail-closed behavior is proven

## Notes
- Use fixture data instead of ad hoc inline setup where possible
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- service-level tests cover major modes
- warnings and status summaries are asserted
- fail-closed behavior is proven
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Use fixture data instead of ad hoc inline setup where possible

title: Add adaptation persistence and idempotency tests
description:
Verify that adaptation writes, session-scoped updates, reset operations, and duplicate interaction handling behave exactly as intended. This task exists because adaptive systems drift subtly if storage semantics are loose.

## Acceptance Criteria
- persist=true and persist=false paths are tested
- duplicate interactions are handled predictably
- reset behavior is proven

## Notes
- Include corruption and partial-write style failure thinking where practical
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- persist=true and persist=false paths are tested
- duplicate interactions are handled predictably
- reset behavior is proven
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Include corruption and partial-write style failure thinking where practical
close_reason: Validated existing adaptation persistence, idempotency, duplicate-interaction, and reset coverage

title: Add CLI, REST, and MCP transport parity tests
description:
Prove that the three promised interfaces expose the same inner behavior even if their envelopes differ. This is the repo-wide check against transport drift.

## Acceptance Criteria
- parity tests exist for compose and explain flows
- major error modes map consistently
- transport glue is regression-tested

## Notes
- Compare structural JSON payloads and key warnings rather than only HTTP status or CLI exit codes
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- parity tests exist for compose and explain flows
- major error modes map consistently
- transport glue is regression-tested
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Compare structural JSON payloads and key warnings rather than only HTTP status or CLI exit codes

title: Align operator docs, runbooks, and examples with PLAN and AGENTS
description:
Write or tighten operator-facing docs so the repo tells the truth about boundaries, workflows, and failure modes. This is where the implementation and docs stop diverging from the actual contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- docs describe real repo boundaries
- examples match actual commands and config
- runbook covers degraded and fail-closed cases

## Notes
- Keep docs explicit that soul is not auth, identify, or registry authority
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- docs describe real repo boundaries
- examples match actual commands and config
- runbook covers degraded and fail-closed cases
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep docs explicit that soul is not auth, identify, or registry authority

title: Define CI-facing test commands and fixture execution flow
description:
Package the test suite into predictable commands and fixture execution flows so later implementation work has a stable definition of done. This task supports future swarm execution and review rounds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- canonical test commands are defined
- fixture setup flow is documented
- CI integration path is straightforward

## Notes
- Make it obvious how to run fast unit tests vs slower parity/integration suites
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- canonical test commands are defined
- fixture setup flow is documented
- CI integration path is straightforward
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Make it obvious how to run fast unit tests vs slower parity/integration suites

title: Add error mapping and exit-code contract tests
description:
Test the transport-facing failure contract directly so revoked, degraded, validation, storage, and template failures do not drift as interfaces evolve. This bead focuses on exact exit/status/result semantics rather than successful compose payload parity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- tests cover CLI exit codes for major SoulError categories
- HTTP status and body mapping are asserted for the same cases
- MCP tool failures are asserted for the same cases

## Notes
- Include revoked/fail-closed, degraded-but-allowed, config invalid, storage failure, and template failure paths
- Focus on exact semantics, not only human-readable strings
- Keep fixtures aligned so the same root cause is exercised through each transport
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- tests cover CLI exit codes for major SoulError categories
- HTTP status and body mapping are asserted for the same cases
- MCP tool failures are asserted for the same cases
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Include revoked/fail-closed, degraded-but-allowed, config invalid, storage failure, and template failure paths
- Focus on exact semantics, not only human-readable strings
- Keep fixtures aligned so the same root cause is exercised through each transport

title: Add cache invalidation and corrupt-cache recovery tests
description:
Exercise the disposable-cache contract directly: cache hits when provenance is unchanged, invalidation when authoritative inputs move, and safe fallback when cache entries are stale or corrupt. This bead exists because cache bugs in soul would silently distort behavior if left untested.

## Acceptance Criteria
- tests prove cache hits only happen for matching authoritative provenance
- changed config/identity/registry/adaptation data invalidates cached results
- corrupt cache content falls back to fresh composition with warnings

## Notes
- Include at least one case where registry timestamps change without broader payload drift
- Assert that cache failure never masks revoked or suspended standing changes
- Favor temporary isolated stores so recovery behavior is reproducible
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- tests prove cache hits only happen for matching authoritative provenance
- changed config/identity/registry/adaptation data invalidates cached results
- corrupt cache content falls back to fresh composition with warnings
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Include at least one case where registry timestamps change without broader payload drift
- Assert that cache failure never masks revoked or suspended standing changes
- Favor temporary isolated stores so recovery behavior is reproducible

title: Add template safety and missing-section rendering tests
description:
Prove that template rendering behaves safely when optional sections are absent and when user-originated text needs escaping. This bead targets the failure class where prompt rendering succeeds technically but leaks malformed or unsafe content into the runtime contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- template tests cover omitted optional sections
- escaping rules are asserted for user-originated free text
- malformed output shapes are caught before transport parity tests run

## Notes
- Include relationship notes and commitment titles in escaping cases
- Verify both prefix and full-context renderers, not only one template
- Make snapshots easy to diff when spacing or headings regress
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- template tests cover omitted optional sections
- escaping rules are asserted for user-originated free text
- malformed output shapes are caught before transport parity tests run
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Include relationship notes and commitment titles in escaping cases
- Verify both prefix and full-context renderers, not only one template
- Make snapshots easy to diff when spacing or headings regress

title: Foundation and runtime contract
description:
Establish the repo skeleton and runtime rules for agents-soul so the rest of the implementation has a clean base. This epic covers workspace structure, config loading, application wiring, error semantics, and deterministic foundations that other layers depend on.

## Success Criteria
- crate layout and runtime contract are defined
- downstream domain and service work has a stable base
- failure semantics are explicit rather than ad hoc

## Notes
- Keep boundaries aligned with PLAN.md and AGENTS.md
- Avoid leaking transport concerns into the core runtime
- Root concern: one agent, one session, one compose request
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- crate layout and runtime contract are defined
- downstream domain and service work has a stable base
- failure semantics are explicit rather than ad hoc
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep boundaries aligned with PLAN.md and AGENTS.md
- Avoid leaking transport concerns into the core runtime
- Root concern: one agent, one session, one compose request
close_reason: Foundation/runtime contract is defined, validated, and all child work is complete

title: Bootstrap crate layout and dependency boundaries
description:
Create the initial Rust crate and module layout for agents-soul, including the separation between domain, services, readers, adaptation, storage, cli, api, and mcp. This task exists to stop architectural drift early and make later work land in the right module from day one.

## Acceptance Criteria
- module layout matches the planned architecture
- no core logic is trapped in transport modules
- future tasks have a clear write surface

## Notes
- Sketch lib/main split
- Reserve domain for contracts and services for orchestration
- Add placeholder modules where necessary so later work does not invent structure ad hoc
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- module layout matches the planned architecture
- no core logic is trapped in transport modules
- future tasks have a clear write surface
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Sketch lib/main split
- Reserve domain for contracts and services for orchestration
- Add placeholder modules where necessary so later work does not invent structure ad hoc

title: Implement soul config loading and validation
description:
Load soul.toml from a workspace path, validate required fields, and materialize defaults for optional settings. This is the first hard contract at session start because every compose run begins from config.

## Acceptance Criteria
- invalid config fails explicitly
- default values are applied deterministically
- workspace path semantics are documented in code

## Notes
- Include limits, identity/registry source locations, and adaptation knobs
- Validation errors should be actionable for operators
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- invalid config fails explicitly
- default values are applied deterministically
- workspace path semantics are documented in code
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Include limits, identity/registry source locations, and adaptation knobs
- Validation errors should be actionable for operators

title: Define error taxonomy and app dependency container
description:
Create the shared error surface and dependency container that the CLI, REST, and MCP layers will use. The point is to have one vocabulary for invalid config, unavailable upstream inputs, revoked standing, storage failures, and template failures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SoulError or equivalent is centralized
- dependency container is explicit
- transports can map core errors consistently

## Notes
- Keep error types aligned with plan boundary contract
- Leave room for degraded vs fail-closed distinction
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- SoulError or equivalent is centralized with explicit buckets for request validation, local config failure, degradable upstream unavailable, upstream broken, fail-closed standing, storage, template, and internal/runtime failure
- AppDeps is explicit and owns config loading, identity reading, registry reading, adaptive-state loading, template rendering, clock generation, and hashing/stable-serialization helpers
- ComposeService and transports consume injected dependencies instead of instantiating readers or loaders ad hoc
- transports can map core errors consistently
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep transport mapping downstream from the domain error types
- Do not let services call config loaders or construct IdentityReader/RegistryReader directly
- Leave room for degraded vs fail-closed distinction

title: Add tracing, hashing, and deterministic utility helpers
description:
Implement common utility helpers such as tracing setup, content hashing for provenance/cache keys, and deterministic ordering helpers. These are cross-cutting primitives needed before snapshot tests and provenance are trustworthy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- tracing can be initialized consistently
- hashing helpers exist for config/adaptation/input provenance
- deterministic ordering helpers are available

## Notes
- Prefer stable ordering for lists rendered into prompts
- Provenance hashes should not depend on incidental field order
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- tracing can be initialized consistently
- hashing helpers exist for config/adaptation/input provenance
- deterministic ordering helpers are available
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Prefer stable ordering for lists rendered into prompts
- Provenance hashes should not depend on incidental field order
close_reason: Validated tracing, hashing, and deterministic helpers are implemented and test-backed

title: Map SoulError into CLI exit codes, HTTP statuses, and MCP failures
description:
Take the shared SoulError taxonomy and finish the contract all the way out to every transport surface. This bead owns CLI exit-code mapping, HTTP status/body mapping, and MCP tool failure semantics so transports can stay thin without inventing their own interpretations of revoked, degraded, validation, storage, or template failures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- a shared mapping exists from SoulError to CLI exit codes
- REST handlers can map the same errors to stable HTTP statuses and response bodies
- MCP tools can report the same failures with predictable codes and messages

## Notes
- Use the exit-code contract from PLAN section 27.5
- Revoked/fail-closed must stay distinct from generic validation or upstream-unavailable cases
- Keep mapping logic centralized so parity and failure tests target one place
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- a shared mapping exists from SoulError to CLI exit codes
- REST handlers can map the same errors to stable HTTP statuses and response bodies
- MCP tools can report the same failures with predictable codes and messages
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Use the exit-code contract from PLAN section 27.5
- Revoked/fail-closed must stay distinct from generic validation or upstream-unavailable cases
- Keep mapping logic centralized so parity and failure tests target one place

title: Implement workspace config patching and atomic soul.toml persistence
description:
Add the inner service that applies validated trait/style/heuristic patches to soul.toml and writes them back safely. This keeps configure and update-traits flows out of transport handlers and makes baseline authoring deterministic, auditable, and testable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- validated trait/style/heuristic patches can be applied to SoulConfig
- soul.toml writes are atomic and deterministic
- configure/update-traits transports can reuse one inner service

## Notes
- Reject partial writes that would corrupt workspace config
- Preserve comments and stable ordering only if the chosen authoring approach can do so safely; otherwise document rewrite semantics clearly
- Keep validation aligned with the same rules used by initial config loading
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- validated trait/style/heuristic patches can be applied to SoulConfig
- soul.toml writes are atomic and deterministic
- configure/update-traits transports can reuse one inner service
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Reject partial writes that would corrupt workspace config
- Preserve comments and stable ordering only if the chosen authoring approach can do so safely; otherwise document rewrite semantics clearly
- Keep validation aligned with the same rules used by initial config loading

title: Remove silent default-config fallback from compose bootstrap
description:
Validation found that src/services/compose.rs currently fabricates a default SoulConfig when soul.toml is missing. That conflicts with the frozen foundation contract: missing local config is an explicit local failure, while degraded and baseline-only modes are reserved for partial or unavailable upstream identity and registry inputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- compose fails with an explicit SoulError when workspace soul.toml is missing or unreadable
- the inner runtime no longer treats missing config as an implicit default-profile path
- tests prove cache stays optional while config remains required

## Notes
- Keep operator-facing config errors actionable
- This bug is about the inner runtime contract, not transport-specific error mapping

title: Make AppDeps authoritative for compose bootstrap
description:
Validation found that the current foundation code still treats AppDeps as a thin wrapper around prebuilt services instead of the single construction boundary promised by PLAN section 60. `src/services/compose.rs` still calls config loading and adaptation loading directly, while `src/app/runtime.rs` ignores its own config/deps and jumps straight to the CLI stub.

## Acceptance Criteria
- the inner compose path receives a real AppDeps boundary for config loading, source readers, adaptation state, template rendering, clock, and hashing helpers
- `ComposeService` stops instantiating or calling config/adaptation loaders ad hoc
- `SoulRuntime` no longer drops injected config/deps when dispatching into the runtime entrypoint
- tests prove the runtime boundary stays transport-thin and dependency-injected

## Notes
- This is a foundation contract bug, not new feature work
- Keep transport mapping downstream from the core dependency boundary

title: CLI, REST, and MCP interfaces
description:
Expose the compose engine through the operational interfaces promised by the plan. This epic covers CLI commands, optional REST endpoints, MCP tools, and the parity layer that keeps them behaviorally consistent.

## Success Criteria
- all required surfaces are exposed
- transport mapping is thin and consistent
- parity expectations are explicit

## Notes
- Interfaces are not allowed to fork core behavior
- They should all delegate to the same inner services
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- all required surfaces are exposed
- transport mapping is thin and consistent
- parity expectations are explicit
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Interfaces are not allowed to fork core behavior
- They should all delegate to the same inner services

title: Implement CLI compose and inspect commands
description:
Expose the core read-only human-operator flows through the CLI so the repo is usable before any separate UI exists. This task now focuses on compose and inspect because those are the highest-signal operator entry points and should be stabilized before mutation commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI compose command exists
- CLI inspect command exists
- inspect supports focused views such as traits-only and heuristics-only via shared projections
- both commands delegate into shared services and support JSON-friendly output

## Notes
- Keep compose/inspect transport-thin
- Follow the same BehavioralContext and explain contracts used elsewhere
- Do not fork separate logic paths for inspect flags beyond projection selection
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- CLI compose command exists
- CLI inspect command exists
- inspect supports focused views such as traits-only and heuristics-only via shared projections
- both commands delegate into shared services and support JSON-friendly output
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep compose/inspect transport-thin
- Follow the same BehavioralContext and explain contracts used elsewhere
- Do not fork separate logic paths for inspect flags beyond projection selection

title: Implement MCP traits, heuristics, and trait-update tools
description:
Complete the MCP inspection and authoring surface by exposing trait and heuristic reads plus the bounded trait-update tool. This bead is important for agent-native tooling because it lets a caller inspect and tune soul state without going through a separate HTTP or CLI shell.

## Acceptance Criteria
- MCP tools exist for trait read, heuristic read, and bounded trait update
- tools reuse shared inspect/config services
- MCP-side failures map through the common transport error contract

## Notes
- Keep update semantics bounded to what soul actually owns
- Distinguish read-only inspection tools from mutation tools clearly in naming and payload shape
- Avoid duplicating config patch validation in the MCP layer
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- MCP tools exist for trait read, heuristic read, and bounded trait update
- tools reuse shared inspect/config services
- MCP-side failures map through the common transport error contract
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep update semantics bounded to what soul actually owns
- Distinguish read-only inspection tools from mutation tools clearly in naming and payload shape
- Avoid duplicating config patch validation in the MCP layer

title: Implement REST compose and explain endpoints
description:
Expose the read-only HTTP endpoints for compose and explain without forking core logic. This task now focuses on the high-value read paths first so API behavior can stabilize before adding mutation endpoints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- REST compose endpoint exists
- REST explain endpoint exists
- handlers map core errors consistently and stay thin

## Notes
- Keep request/response envelopes predictable
- Use the same inner services as CLI and MCP
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- REST compose endpoint exists
- REST explain endpoint exists
- handlers map core errors consistently and stay thin
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep request/response envelopes predictable
- Use the same inner services as CLI and MCP

title: Implement MCP compose, prefix, and explain tools
description:
Expose the primary read-only MCP surface for session startup and operator debugging. This task now focuses on compose, get-prefix, and explain style tools because they are the first runtime integration points that must be correct.

## Acceptance Criteria
- MCP compose tool exists
- MCP prefix/explain tools exist
- tool results map cleanly to core service responses

## Notes
- Read-path MCP tools come before mutation tools
- Keep naming and payload shape aligned with plan contracts
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- MCP compose tool exists
- MCP prefix/explain tools exist
- tool results map cleanly to core service responses
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Read-path MCP tools come before mutation tools
- Keep naming and payload shape aligned with plan contracts

title: Implement transport mapping and parity glue
description:
Unify request mapping and response parity across CLI, REST, and MCP so there is one semantic contract even if transports differ syntactically. This task is about consistency and regression resistance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- shared mapping helpers exist where needed
- parity expectations are codified
- transport-specific drift is minimized

## Notes
- Build toward byte-level or structural parity where practical
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- shared mapping helpers exist where needed
- parity expectations are codified
- transport-specific drift is minimized
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Build toward byte-level or structural parity where practical

title: Implement CLI configure, record, and reset commands
description:
Expose the mutating operator flows through the CLI: configure baseline state, record interaction outcomes, and reset adaptive state. These commands are operationally important but should land after the read-path commands are stable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI configure command exists
- CLI record command exists
- CLI reset command exists and maps core errors predictably

## Notes
- Keep mutations explicit and operator-visible
- Reuse shared service logic instead of embedding state logic in clap handlers
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- CLI configure command exists
- CLI record command exists
- CLI reset command exists and maps core errors predictably
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep mutations explicit and operator-visible
- Reuse shared service logic instead of embedding state logic in clap handlers

title: Implement REST traits, interactions, and reset endpoints
description:
Expose the HTTP mutation endpoints needed for programmatic management of soul state, including trait/config adjustments where allowed, interaction recording, and reset flows. This splits the API write surface away from the read-only compose/explain endpoints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- REST endpoints exist for interaction recording and reset flows
- any config/trait mutation endpoints are explicit and validated
- write-path handlers remain thin over shared services

## Notes
- Keep write endpoints separate from compose/explain to simplify parity testing
- Surface validation failures clearly for operators and clients
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- REST endpoints exist for interaction recording and reset flows
- any config/trait mutation endpoints are explicit and validated
- write-path handlers remain thin over shared services
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep write endpoints separate from compose/explain to simplify parity testing
- Surface validation failures clearly for operators and clients

title: Implement MCP interaction-record and reset tools
description:
Expose the MCP mutation tools needed after a session runs: record interaction outcomes, reset adaptive state, and other bounded maintenance actions promised by the plan. This keeps runtime session tooling symmetrical without overloading the read-path MCP beads.

## Acceptance Criteria
- MCP record-interaction tool exists
- MCP reset tool exists
- mutation tool outputs map cleanly to shared service results

## Notes
- Keep tool semantics explicit so session runtimes do not accidentally mutate hidden state
- Mutation tools should remain bounded and auditable
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- MCP record-interaction tool exists
- MCP reset tool exists
- mutation tool outputs map cleanly to shared service results
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep tool semantics explicit so session runtimes do not accidentally mutate hidden state
- Mutation tools should remain bounded and auditable
close_reason: Implemented MCP interaction-record and reset tool seam with focused validation

title: Implement CLI explain command and prefix-only compose flow
description:
Finish the remaining read-side CLI surface promised by the plan: explicit explain output and prefix-only compose output. This bead keeps those variants transport-thin while ensuring the CLI can expose the same context, warnings, and failure semantics as other surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI explain command exists
- compose supports a prefix-only variant without bypassing core services
- CLI output and failure semantics match the shared transport contract

## Notes
- Prefix-only is still a compose-path view, not a shortcut that skips mode resolution
- Explain output should reuse the inspect projection rather than rebuilding custom strings in clap handlers
- Exit codes must come from the shared error mapping layer
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- CLI explain command exists
- compose supports a prefix-only variant without bypassing core services
- CLI output and failure semantics match the shared transport contract
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Prefix-only is still a compose-path view, not a shortcut that skips mode resolution
- Explain output should reuse the inspect projection rather than rebuilding custom strings in clap handlers
- Exit codes must come from the shared error mapping layer

title: Implement REST traits and heuristics read endpoints
description:
Expose the structured read-only inspection surfaces over HTTP, covering traits and heuristics views that operators and future UIs will consume. This bead separates those narrower inspection endpoints from the heavier compose/explain endpoints while keeping them on the same inner projection contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- REST traits endpoint exists
- REST heuristics endpoint exists
- handlers expose the shared inspect projection with stable HTTP error mapping

## Notes
- Keep response envelopes compatible with the broader compose/explain API style
- Do not fork business logic in handlers just because payloads are smaller
- These endpoints should be suitable for a future dedicated UI repo

Done when:
- REST traits endpoint exists
- REST heuristics endpoint exists
- handlers expose the shared inspect projection with stable HTTP error mapping

Implementation notes:
- Keep response envelopes compatible with the broader compose/explain API style
- Do not fork business logic in handlers just because payloads are smaller
- These endpoints should be suitable for a future dedicated UI repo

title: Identify/registry readers and normalization
description:
Build the inbound data path from agents-identify and agents-registry into a normalized input bundle. This epic owns reader contracts, degraded/offline behavior, normalization, and warnings that make missing upstream truth visible instead of implicit.

## Success Criteria
- identify and registry read paths are defined
- normalized inputs are deterministic
- degraded behavior is explicit and testable

## Notes
- Soul is a consumer of upstream truth only
- Missing sources may degrade output but must not cause fabricated truth
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- identify and registry read paths are defined
- normalized inputs are deterministic
- degraded behavior is explicit and testable
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Soul is a consumer of upstream truth only
- Missing sources may degrade output but must not cause fabricated truth
close_reason: Completed: identify/registry readers, deterministic normalization, and degraded behavior are implemented and validated via closed child beads and focused tests

title: Implement agents-identify reader contract and adapters
description:
Define and implement the reader interface for pulling local identity-continuity signals from agents-identify or preloaded snapshots. This includes commitments, preferences, relationship markers, and identify health state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- identify reader contract exists
- adapter can read real or fixture input
- identify state is surfaced explicitly to normalization

## Notes
- Missing identify data may degrade behavior but should not fabricate commitments or preferences
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- identify reader contract exists
- adapter can read real or fixture input
- identify state is surfaced explicitly to normalization
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Missing identify data may degrade behavior but should not fabricate commitments or preferences

title: Implement agents-registry reader contract and adapters
description:
Define and implement the reader interface for pulling standing and reputation signals from agents-registry or preloaded fixtures. This reader is the source for active/suspended/revoked state and reputation-derived caution.

## Acceptance Criteria
- registry reader contract exists
- standing and reputation are modeled explicitly
- adapter supports real and fixture input

## Notes
- Registry standing is authoritative for fail-closed vs restricted behavior
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- registry reader contract exists
- standing and reputation are modeled explicitly
- adapter supports real and fixture input
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Registry standing is authoritative for fail-closed vs restricted behavior

title: Normalize config, identify, registry, and adaptation into one input bundle
description:
Merge all upstream inputs into a normalized representation with deterministic defaults and ordering. This task is where optional data becomes explicit absence rather than hidden null behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- normalized bundle exists
- missing sources are represented deliberately
- deterministic ordering is preserved

## Notes
- Normalize once, then share downstream across compose and explain paths
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- normalized bundle exists
- missing sources are represented deliberately
- deterministic ordering is preserved
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Normalize once, then share downstream across compose and explain paths
close_reason: Completed: normalized input bundle is implemented and validated by reader/normalization tests

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text: **Summary:** implemented normalized upstream input bundling and validated deterministic behavior

title: Implement degraded and offline policy handling
description:
Codify how soul behaves when identify or registry is unavailable, malformed, or partially present. The goal is to make degraded behavior intentional, operator-visible, and aligned with the plan rather than accidental.

## Acceptance Criteria
- degraded/offline policy is explicit
- warnings surface upstream gaps
- illegal fallbacks are blocked

## Notes
- Revoked must still fail closed
- Registry-unavailable behavior should follow policy, not ad hoc guesswork
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- degraded/offline policy is explicit
- warnings surface upstream gaps
- illegal fallbacks are blocked
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Revoked must still fail closed
- Registry-unavailable behavior should follow policy, not ad hoc guesswork
close_reason: Completed: degraded/offline policy is implemented in normalization, compose mode resolution, and warning derivation, with focused tests passing

title: Implement reader precedence across explicit input, live sources, and cached snapshots
description:
Codify how soul chooses upstream data before normalization begins: explicit input first, live reads second, and cached snapshots only as bounded fallback. This bead makes source selection deterministic and keeps cache use visible rather than accidental.

## Acceptance Criteria
- explicit input, live read, and cached snapshot precedence is explicit
- reader selection behavior is deterministic and testable
- normalization receives a source-selection result that preserves provenance

## Notes
- Prefer explicit input when supplied by caller
- Cache is fallback or acceleration only, never hidden authority
- Surface which source path actually won so degraded behavior remains explainable
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- explicit input, live read, and cached snapshot precedence is explicit
- reader selection behavior is deterministic and testable
- normalization receives a source-selection result that preserves provenance
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Prefer explicit input when supplied by caller
- Cache is fallback or acceleration only, never hidden authority
- Surface which source path actually won so degraded behavior remains explainable

title: Implement Foundation and runtime contract
description: Execution task generated from workflow plan for soul-1ip.

title: Storage, persistence, and fixtures
description:
Add the persistence surfaces agents-soul needs for bounded adaptation and reproducible testing. This epic covers SQLite schema, adaptation state storage, disposable cache behavior, and fixtures that let the repo prove deterministic output.

## Success Criteria
- persistence model is explicit
- adaptation state is durable where intended
- test fixtures exist for repeatable runs

## Notes
- Cache can exist for speed but must never become authority
- Persistence must preserve baseline vs adaptive override separation
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- persistence model is explicit
- adaptation state is durable where intended
- test fixtures exist for repeatable runs
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Cache can exist for speed but must never become authority
- Persistence must preserve baseline vs adaptive override separation

title: Create SQLite schema and migrations for adaptive state
description:
Design the SQLite schema used for adaptive overrides, interaction logs, and any durable behavioral adjustments. The schema should preserve the baseline-vs-adaptive split and support reset/idempotency behaviors from the plan.

## Acceptance Criteria
- migration set creates required tables and indexes
- schema supports bounded adaptation and interaction logs
- reset semantics are possible without rewriting baseline config

## Notes
- Keep schema minimal for v1
- Separate append-only interaction evidence from effective override state
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- migration set creates required tables and indexes
- schema supports bounded adaptation and interaction logs
- reset semantics are possible without rewriting baseline config
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep schema minimal for v1
- Separate append-only interaction evidence from effective override state

title: Implement adaptation store load, persist, and reset paths
description:
Implement the storage layer that reads and writes effective adaptive state. This task makes the adaptation system real by supporting session reads, durable writes, and targeted/full reset behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- store can load effective state
- persist=true writes durable changes
- reset paths clear adaptive state predictably

## Notes
- Persisted state must stay within configured drift limits
- Reset should not damage baseline config
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- store can load effective state
- persist=true writes durable changes
- reset paths clear adaptive state predictably
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Persisted state must stay within configured drift limits
- Reset should not damage baseline config

title: Implement disposable context cache with explicit non-authority semantics
description:
Add an optional cache for rendered or intermediate compose results to improve performance without changing authority boundaries. The cache must be clearly disposable and invalidated by relevant provenance inputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- cache layer exists behind an explicit interface
- cache keys derive from stable provenance inputs
- cache failure cannot corrupt or replace source truth

## Notes
- Treat cache miss and cache corruption as recoverable
- Never let cache hide revoked/suspended state changes
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- cache layer exists behind an explicit interface
- cache keys derive from stable provenance inputs
- cache failure cannot corrupt or replace source truth
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Treat cache miss and cache corruption as recoverable
- Never let cache hide revoked/suspended state changes

title: Build fixture loaders and storage-oriented test harnesses
description:
Create reusable test harness helpers and fixtures for storage-backed tests so adaptation and persistence can be exercised deterministically. This includes fixture setup, temporary databases, and canned identity/registry snapshots.

## Acceptance Criteria
- tests can spin up isolated storage fixtures
- fixture helpers reduce duplication
- storage tests are reproducible

## Notes
- Favor helpers that can be reused by integration and transport parity tests
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- tests can spin up isolated storage fixtures
- fixture helpers reduce duplication
- storage tests are reproducible
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Favor helpers that can be reused by integration and transport parity tests

title: Implement adaptive state reads and effective override materialization
description:
Focus the adaptation storage work on reading persisted adaptive state and materializing the effective override set that compose consumes. This read path should be deterministic, bounded, and easy to use from the compose service.

## Acceptance Criteria
- adaptive read path exists
- effective override materialization exists
- read path respects configured drift limits

## Notes
- Keep read semantics cleanly separated from mutation semantics
- This bead should unblock compose work without requiring full mutation paths first
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- adaptive read path exists
- effective override materialization exists
- read path respects configured drift limits
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep read semantics cleanly separated from mutation semantics
- This bead should unblock compose work without requiring full mutation paths first

title: Implement adaptive writes, reset semantics, and idempotent persistence
description:
Focus the adaptation storage work on persisting updates, clearing state, and handling idempotent or duplicate writes safely. This bead isolates mutation semantics from read semantics so persistence behavior can be tested directly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- durable adaptive write path exists
- reset semantics are implemented
- duplicate or repeated writes are handled predictably
- durable writes are clearly separated from session-scoped or persist=false effects

## Notes
- Keep mutation rules explicit and testable
- Preserve baseline config while modifying adaptive state only
- Manual operator config must remain authoritative over learned adaptive state during persistence
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- durable adaptive write path exists
- reset semantics are implemented
- duplicate or repeated writes are handled predictably
- durable writes are clearly separated from session-scoped or persist=false effects
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep mutation rules explicit and testable
- Preserve baseline config while modifying adaptive state only
- Manual operator config must remain authoritative over learned adaptive state during persistence

title: Implement cache invalidation and corrupt-cache recovery semantics
description:
Finish the cache contract by deciding exactly when cached compose data becomes stale and how the runtime recovers from corruption. This bead keeps the cache disposable by keying invalidation to provenance inputs and forcing safe fallback to fresh composition on any doubt.

## Acceptance Criteria
- cache invalidation rules cover config, identify, registry, and adaptation changes
- corrupt cache entries force safe recomposition
- cache freshness can be reasoned about from provenance data

## Notes
- Invalidate on authoritative input changes, not on transport-specific envelopes
- Treat unreadable or malformed cache content as a warning plus fallback, never as authority
- Keep recovery paths cheap enough that cache failure is operationally boring
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- cache invalidation rules cover config, identify, registry, and adaptation changes
- corrupt cache entries force safe recomposition
- cache freshness can be reasoned about from provenance data
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Invalidate on authoritative input changes, not on transport-specific envelopes
- Treat unreadable or malformed cache content as a warning plus fallback, never as authority
- Keep recovery paths cheap enough that cache failure is operationally boring

title: Compose engine and behavior shaping
description:
Implement the core engine that resolves legal compose mode and turns normalized inputs into an effective behavioral profile, rules, warnings, and final BehavioralContext. This epic is the heart of agents-soul.

## Success Criteria
- compose mode logic is explicit
- behavior shaping is bounded and deterministic
- BehavioralContext build path is centralized

## Notes
- Fail closed on revoked standing
- Restricted and degraded modes must visibly reduce autonomy
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- compose mode logic is explicit
- behavior shaping is bounded and deterministic
- BehavioralContext build path is centralized
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Fail closed on revoked standing
- Restricted and degraded modes must visibly reduce autonomy

title: Implement compose mode resolver
description:
Resolve ComposeMode from normalized inputs using the contract in the plan: revoked -> fail closed, suspended -> restricted, degraded inputs -> degraded or baseline-only, healthy upstream state -> normal. This task is the legal gate for all later rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- compose mode resolver exists
- revoked/suspended/degraded/baseline-only cases are explicit
- no transport chooses the mode manually

## Notes
- Keep the resolver small and highly testable
- This is the boundary between upstream truth and behavioral shaping
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- compose mode resolver exists
- precedence matches PLAN section 60.5 exactly, including registry unavailable plus identity absent resolving to BaselineOnly
- revoked, suspended, degraded, and baseline-only cases are explicit and unit-tested
- no transport chooses the mode manually
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep the resolver small and highly testable
- Offline policy applies when registry truth is missing, but identity absence still caps the result at baseline-only
- This is the boundary between upstream truth and behavioral shaping

title: Implement effective profile derivation and drift clamping
description:
Derive the effective trait profile by layering baseline config, degraded/restricted modifiers, and bounded adaptive overrides. This is where the system changes how direct, warm, initiative-heavy, or risk-tolerant the agent should be.

## Acceptance Criteria
- effective profile derivation exists
- drift is clamped to configured limits
- restricted/degraded modes visibly reduce risky autonomy

## Notes
- Preserve determinism
- Keep the baseline profile inspectable alongside the effective one
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- effective profile derivation exists
- drift is clamped to configured limits
- restricted/degraded modes visibly reduce risky autonomy
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Preserve determinism
- Keep the baseline profile inspectable alongside the effective one

title: Implement communication rules, decision rules, commitments, and relationship shaping
description:
Turn normalized inputs and the effective profile into concrete runtime guidance: communication rules, decision heuristics, commitment strings, and relationship context. This bead focuses on shaping the usable behavioral instructions once warning derivation has already been handled by dedicated helpers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- rule derivation helpers exist
- commitments, relationships, status state, and reputation can affect behavior
- pending and retired registry states shape behavior explicitly rather than implicitly
- the resulting guidance is concrete enough for prompt rendering and inspect output

## Notes
- Rules should be concrete and runtime-usable, not vague adjectives
- Reuse dedicated warning helpers rather than mixing warning assembly into every rule path
- Keep commitments and relationship shaping deterministic for snapshot testing
- Low reputation, pending standing, and retired standing should produce explicit caution or readonly framing where required by the plan
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- rule derivation helpers exist
- commitments, relationships, status state, and reputation can affect behavior
- pending and retired registry states shape behavior explicitly rather than implicitly
- the resulting guidance is concrete enough for prompt rendering and inspect output
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Rules should be concrete and runtime-usable, not vague adjectives
- Reuse dedicated warning helpers rather than mixing warning assembly into every rule path
- Keep commitments and relationship shaping deterministic for snapshot testing
- Low reputation, pending standing, and retired standing should produce explicit caution or readonly framing where required by the plan

title: Implement compose service orchestration
description:
Build the central orchestration path that loads inputs, resolves compose mode, coordinates profile/rule/render helpers, and returns the final BehavioralContext. This task now focuses on orchestration and assembly, with status/provenance and fail-closed helper paths split into dedicated beads.

## Acceptance Criteria
- compose service orchestrates the full happy-path build
- helper boundaries for status/provenance and fail-closed behavior are integrated
- compose path is shared by transports

## Notes
- Keep orchestration thin over helper services
- Do not bury fail-closed behavior in transport code
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- compose service orchestrates the full happy-path build
- helper boundaries for status/provenance and fail-closed behavior are integrated
- compose path is shared by transports
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep orchestration thin over helper services
- Do not bury fail-closed behavior in transport code

title: Implement status summary and provenance builders
description:
Build the helper paths that summarize upstream input status and construct provenance metadata such as config/adaptation/input hashes and source timestamps. This information is consumed by BehavioralContext, explain output, and cache invalidation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- status summary helper exists
- provenance builder exists
- degraded/upstream visibility is preserved in structured output

## Notes
- Keep provenance tied to real normalized inputs
- Hashes should be stable across identical input bundles
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- status summary helper exists
- provenance builder exists
- degraded/upstream visibility is preserved in structured output
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep provenance tied to real normalized inputs
- Hashes should be stable across identical input bundles
close_reason: Validated existing status/provenance helpers and added focused coverage

title: Implement fail-closed and restricted context builders
description:
Provide dedicated helper builders for revoked and restricted operating modes so these critical behaviors are obvious, testable, and not hidden inside generic rendering branches. This task isolates the high-risk control-flow paths from the normal compose path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- fail-closed builder exists for revoked standing
- restricted-mode helper path exists
- warnings and reduced autonomy rules are explicit in these paths

## Notes
- Treat revoked as a first-class branch, not just another warning
- Keep helper outputs easy to snapshot-test
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- fail-closed builder exists for revoked standing
- restricted-mode helper path exists
- warnings and reduced autonomy rules are explicit in these paths
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Treat revoked as a first-class branch, not just another warning
- Keep helper outputs easy to snapshot-test

title: Implement interaction reducer and bounded adaptive override computation
description:
Build the adaptation engine that turns interaction evidence into bounded trait and heuristic overrides plus transparent notes. This is the missing logic between raw interaction records and the effective behavioral profile, and it must clamp drift instead of letting storage or transports invent adaptation behavior ad hoc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- interaction evidence can produce bounded trait overrides
- heuristic overrides and adaptation notes are derived explicitly
- reducer output is suitable for durable persistence and later composition

## Notes
- Keep drift-clamping logic here rather than scattering it through transports or storage
- Preserve transparency by emitting notes that explain why a durable override exists
- Reducer should be deterministic for the same evidence window and config
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- interaction evidence can produce bounded trait overrides
- heuristic overrides and adaptation notes are derived explicitly
- reducer output is suitable for durable persistence and later composition
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep drift-clamping logic here rather than scattering it through transports or storage
- Preserve transparency by emitting notes that explain why a durable override exists
- Reducer should be deterministic for the same evidence window and config

title: Implement warning derivation, severity ordering, and deduplication
description:
Extract warning generation into a dedicated service that translates upstream problems and risky modes into explicit operator-visible warnings. This bead owns severity ordering and deduplication so the final BehavioralContext is consistent even when multiple sources report overlapping problems.

## Acceptance Criteria
- warning derivation exists as a dedicated helper
- warning severity ordering is deterministic
- duplicate or overlapping warnings collapse cleanly without hiding important state

## Notes
- Warnings should be structured first, rendered later
- Keep revoked and suspended warnings first-class rather than generic strings
- This helper should be reused by compose, inspect, and explain flows
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- warning derivation exists as a dedicated helper
- warning severity ordering is deterministic
- duplicate or overlapping warnings collapse cleanly without hiding important state
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Warnings should be structured first, rendered later
- Keep revoked and suspended warnings first-class rather than generic strings
- This helper should be reused by compose, inspect, and explain flows

title: Domain model and config surface
description:
Define the core types that make soul understandable and deterministic: traits, styles, heuristics, behavioral context, provenance, status, compose request, compose mode, and config structures. These types are the language every service and transport must share.

## Success Criteria
- core structs and enums are defined
- config/default semantics are explicit
- public contracts are stable enough for service and interface layers

## Notes
- Keep naming aligned with PLAN.md
- The domain should express behavior, not transport details
- Revoked/suspended/degraded semantics must be visible in the model
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- core structs and enums are defined
- config/default semantics are explicit
- public contracts are stable enough for service and interface layers
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep naming aligned with PLAN.md
- The domain should express behavior, not transport details
- Revoked/suspended/degraded semantics must be visible in the model
close_reason: Completed: domain/config contract is implemented and validated by domain_contracts + workspace_config_examples

title: Model personality traits, styles, heuristics, and limits
description:
Define the core behavioral data structures for baseline personality, communication style, decision heuristics, and hard operating limits. These types are the raw material used by the compose engine and adaptation layer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- trait/style/heuristic/limit models exist
- fields needed by compose are represented explicitly
- types are suitable for serde and testing

## Notes
- Use domain names that map cleanly to the plan
- Favor explicit fields over vague free-form maps for v1
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- trait/style/heuristic/limit models exist
- fields needed by compose are represented explicitly
- types are suitable for serde and testing
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Use domain names that map cleanly to the plan
- Favor explicit fields over vague free-form maps for v1

title: Define BehavioralContext, status summary, and provenance types
description:
Model the output contract of agents-soul so every transport can expose the same core payload. This includes the BehavioralContext itself plus status summary and provenance information that explain what upstream truth was used.

## Acceptance Criteria
- BehavioralContext fields are explicit
- status and provenance are first-class types
- output contract is stable for snapshots and transports

## Notes
- Output should make degraded or fail-closed state visible to operators and runtimes
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- BehavioralContext fields are explicit
- status and provenance are first-class types
- output contract is stable for snapshots and transports
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Output should make degraded or fail-closed state visible to operators and runtimes

title: Define compose request, compose mode, and normalized input bundle
description:
Represent the session-start request and the normalized input bundle that the compose engine operates on. This task codifies the rule that soul receives one agent and one session, then resolves the legal compose mode from upstream truth.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ComposeRequest exists
- ComposeMode exists with normal/restricted/degraded/baseline-only/fail-closed
- normalized input bundle is explicit

## Notes
- Keep the caller authoritative for agent/session selection
- Soul resolves mode; caller does not choose it
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- ComposeRequest exists
- ComposeMode exists with normal/restricted/degraded/baseline-only/fail-closed
- normalized input bundle is explicit
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep the caller authoritative for agent/session selection
- Soul resolves mode; caller does not choose it

title: Define workspace config examples and default authoring patterns
description:
Provide concrete config examples and defaults that show how a soul workspace should be authored. This task reduces operator ambiguity and makes fixtures and docs consistent with the actual config loader.

## Acceptance Criteria
- example config matches loader expectations
- defaults are documented in repo artifacts
- example workspaces cover common scenarios

## Notes
- Include healthy, degraded, and minimal examples where useful
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- example config matches loader expectations
- defaults are documented in repo artifacts
- example workspaces cover common scenarios
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Include healthy, degraded, and minimal examples where useful

title: Implement Identify/registry readers and normalization
description: Execution task generated from workflow plan for soul-2i7.

title: Rendering and explainability
description:
Render the outputs that the runtime and human operators actually consume: prompt prefix, full context, explain payloads, and provenance-backed snapshots. This epic turns internal models into usable external surfaces.

## Success Criteria
- rendered outputs exist for runtime and operators
- explain surfaces map back to real contributors
- snapshots can lock the behavior contract

## Notes
- Rendering must preserve deterministic ordering
- Explain output should help humans see why a trait or warning exists
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- rendered outputs exist for runtime and operators
- explain surfaces map back to real contributors
- snapshots can lock the behavior contract
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Rendering must preserve deterministic ordering
- Explain output should help humans see why a trait or warning exists

title: Implement prompt prefix renderer
description:
Render the short behavioral prefix that the runtime injects into system prompts at session start. The renderer must preserve mode semantics, keep revoked and suspended outputs visibly distinct, and enforce the configured prompt-prefix length contract without introducing nondeterministic truncation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- prefix renderer exists
- restricted and fail-closed outputs are visibly distinct
- max_prompt_prefix_chars is enforced with deterministic compaction or truncation rules
- ordering is stable for snapshot testing

## Notes
- Keep important warnings near the top
- Avoid prompt inflation when concise output is requested
- Length enforcement must not erase revoked or suspended safety cues first
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- prefix renderer exists
- restricted and fail-closed outputs are visibly distinct
- max_prompt_prefix_chars is enforced with deterministic compaction or truncation rules
- ordering is stable for snapshot testing
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep important warnings near the top
- Avoid prompt inflation when concise output is requested
- Length enforcement must not erase revoked or suspended safety cues first

title: Implement full behavioral context renderer
description:
Render the full context output used for deeper inspection and richer runtime injection. This output should include traits, rules, commitments, relationship context, adaptive notes, and warnings in a deterministic layout.

## Acceptance Criteria
- full context renderer exists
- all relevant sections are rendered or explicitly omitted
- output is stable enough for fixture-based snapshots

## Notes
- Missing optional sections should render as explicit absence, not malformed layout
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- full context renderer exists
- all relevant sections are rendered or explicitly omitted
- output is stable enough for fixture-based snapshots
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Missing optional sections should render as explicit absence, not malformed layout

title: Implement explain response assembly
description:
Provide the final operator-facing explain structure that ties effective traits and warnings back to baseline config, upstream signals, and adaptive contributors. This task now focuses on assembling the outward explain payload from helper inputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- explain payload shape is finalized
- explain assembly uses helper contributors and provenance builders
- operator can see why effective behavior changed

## Notes
- Keep explain assembly thin over dedicated contributor/provenance helpers
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- explain payload shape is finalized
- explain assembly uses helper contributors and provenance builders
- operator can see why effective behavior changed
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Keep explain assembly thin over dedicated contributor/provenance helpers

title: Create snapshot fixtures for normal, restricted, degraded, and fail-closed outputs
description:
Lock the output contract down with canonical fixtures for the main behavior modes. These snapshots are the regression harness for future prompt or rendering changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- fixtures exist for key modes
- snapshot tests can assert stable output
- revoked/suspended cases are included

## Notes
- Include at least one explain/provenance snapshot, not just prompt text
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- fixtures exist for key modes
- snapshot tests can assert stable output
- revoked/suspended cases are included
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Include at least one explain/provenance snapshot, not just prompt text

title: Implement explain contributor extraction helpers
description:
Extract the helper logic that explains why each effective field changed, including baseline sources, degraded/restricted modifiers, upstream signals, and adaptive overrides. This makes explain output more trustworthy and keeps the final assembly bead thin.

## Acceptance Criteria
- contributor helper exists for key behavioral fields
- helper can enumerate baseline vs upstream vs adaptation contributors
- output is deterministic enough for explain snapshots

## Notes
- Prefer structured contributor data before formatting into strings
- Keep helper coverage focused on the fields operators actually inspect
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- contributor helper exists for key behavioral fields
- helper can enumerate baseline vs upstream vs adaptation contributors
- output is deterministic enough for explain snapshots
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Prefer structured contributor data before formatting into strings
- Keep helper coverage focused on the fields operators actually inspect

title: Implement explain-side provenance materialization
description:
Materialize the provenance payload needed by explain and inspect views, including hashes, source fingerprints, and verification timestamps where available. This bead separates provenance materialization from final explain response assembly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- explain-side provenance helper exists
- helper can surface source hashes and timestamps cleanly
- explain assembly can consume the helper without re-deriving provenance

## Notes
- Reuse the same provenance model as BehavioralContext where possible
- Avoid duplicated hash logic across explain and compose paths
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- explain-side provenance helper exists
- helper can surface source hashes and timestamps cleanly
- explain assembly can consume the helper without re-deriving provenance
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Reuse the same provenance model as BehavioralContext where possible
- Avoid duplicated hash logic across explain and compose paths

title: Implement template engine loading, escaping, and missing-section safety
description:
Create the shared template engine that loads built-in templates, applies consistent escaping, and renders missing optional sections safely. This bead makes template safety explicit before renderer-specific code lands, especially for relationship notes, commitments, and other free text that may come from upstream state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- built-in templates load through one engine
- escaping and missing-section behavior is explicit
- renderers can rely on shared template safety rather than ad hoc string assembly

## Notes
- Escape relationship notes, commitments, and other free text before render
- Missing optional sections should omit cleanly without malformed headings or separators
- Keep template names and loading paths stable for snapshot fixtures
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- built-in templates load through one engine
- escaping and missing-section behavior is explicit
- renderers can rely on shared template safety rather than ad hoc string assembly
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Escape relationship notes, commitments, and other free text before render
- Missing optional sections should omit cleanly without malformed headings or separators
- Keep template names and loading paths stable for snapshot fixtures

title: Implement inspect report projections for traits, heuristics, adaptations, and warnings
description:
Build the structured inspect/explain projection that operator-facing commands and endpoints can reuse when they need more than a rendered prompt. This bead owns the view model for effective traits, heuristics, adaptation notes, warnings, and provenance so transports do not reassemble that shape independently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- inspect/explain projection types exist for traits, heuristics, adaptations, warnings, and provenance
- the projection can be reused across CLI, REST, and MCP
- inspect-focused slices such as traits-only and heuristics-only can be derived without rebuilding business logic
- transports no longer need to reconstruct operator-facing inspect payloads themselves

## Notes
- Prefer structured fields over preformatted strings
- Keep effective vs baseline values visible where that distinction matters to operators
- Reuse contributor and provenance helpers rather than recomputing them here
- This projection is the basis for inspect flags, not a separate ad hoc formatter per transport
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- inspect/explain projection types exist for traits, heuristics, adaptations, warnings, and provenance
- the projection can be reused across CLI, REST, and MCP
- inspect-focused slices such as traits-only and heuristics-only can be derived without rebuilding business logic
- transports no longer need to reconstruct operator-facing inspect payloads themselves
notes:
Implementation notes:
- Prefer structured fields over preformatted strings
- Keep effective vs baseline values visible where that distinction matters to operators
- Reuse contributor and provenance helpers rather than recomputing them here
- This projection is the basis for inspect flags, not a separate ad hoc formatter per transport
